HTTP Status Codes Reference

HTTP status codes are standard server responses that indicate the result of a client’s request.They are grouped into five categories based on their purpose and meaning.


Common HTTP Status Codes and Their Meanings
Code Name Description Category
100 Continue The server received the request headers and the client may continue sending the body. Informational Responses (1xx)
101 Switching Protocols The server is switching protocols as requested by the client.
200 OK The request was completed successfully. Successful Responses (2xx)
201 Create A new resource was successfully created.
204 No Content The request succeeded but no content was returned.
301 Moved Permanently The resource has been permanently moved to another URL. Redirection Messages (3xx)
302 Found The resource is temporarily located at a different URL.
304 Not Modified The cached version can still be used.
400 Bad Request The server could not understand the request. Client Error Responses (4xx)
401 Unauthorized Authentication is required to access the resource.
403 Forbidden The client does not have permission to access the resource.
404 Not Found The requested resource could not be found.
500 Internal Server Error The server encountered an unexpected condition. Server Error Responses (5xx)
502 Bad Gateway The server received an invalid response from another server.
503 Service Unavailable The server is temporarily unavailable.
Total Listed 15 status codes across 5 categories
